other_observers: Many people present at MacIntosh Lake on 11/27 included Bill Schmoker, Nancy Gobris, Bill Maynard, Rachel Hopper, Glenn Walbek.; I do not know who originally found this bird.
specific_location: N edge of Lake Loveland, Loveland
description: A large, brown bird, with an immense bill, though smaller than neighboring American White Pelicans (at MacIntosh Lake on 11/27). The bird was mostly brownish above, with a brownish neck and chest, and white belly. On the head, there was a distinctive patch of white feathers, mostly auriculars, that enabled me to be sure that the bird at MacIntosh Lake was the same spent much of the late fall in Larimer and Weld counties.  From above, the open wings had brownish coverts and very dark brown remiges. From below, the whitish greater coverts (both primary and secondary) contrasted with the brownish color of the rest of the wing linings and the dark brown remiges, creating a wing stripe extending from the axillars nearly to the leading edge of the wing.Behaviors: Stretching and sleeping on a tire at Lake Loveland, standing on the beach and flying at MacIntosh Lake.Call: None heardPlumage: Juvenal molting to Basic I
